title: xanthoxalis lyonii
intro: Xanthoxalis lyonii, also known as Wood Sorrel, is a perennial herb native to the western United States and Canada, characterized by its yellow flowers and heart-shaped leaves. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to treat conditions such as diarrhea and wounds. The health benefits of Xanthoxalis lyonii include its ability to act as an antimicrobial agent, reducing the risk of infections, and its potential to help lower blood sugar levels. Xanthoxalis lyonii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional medicine to enhance its effects.
